content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
65	
66	## User Interaction
67	
68	Users provide hints in two places:
69	
70	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
71	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
72	
73	## Agent Roles
74	
75	### Plan Agent
76	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
77	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
78	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
79	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
80	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
81	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
82	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
83	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
84	
85	### Prover Agent
86	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
87	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
88	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
89	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
90	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
91	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
92	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
93	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
94	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
95	
96	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
97	
98	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
99	
100	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
101	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
102	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
103	
104	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
105	
106	### Review Agent
107	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
108	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
109	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
110	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
111	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
112	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
137

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	prover  (iteration 051 — Phase A step 6 *Path 2* / Serre-finiteness scaffolding **instance-driven supremum HModule' consumer + curve specialisation**: append two declarations to `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` `section CoverTotality` (inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`), after iter-050's `subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top_curve` (currently L1161), before `end CoverTotality` (currently L1163): (1) `theorem subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso` — symmetric instance-driven counterpart to iter-048's explicit-argument supr consumer, body `subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover cechToHModuleIso n hn`; (2) `_curve` specialisation. Iter-050 closure verified (this pass): all four iter-050 declarations land cleanly at L1094 (`HasCechToHModuleIso`), L1110 (`cechToHModuleIso`), L1134 (`_top`), L1152 (`_top_curve`); sorry count `9 → 9`; file LOC `1077 → 1165` (+88, slight overage from multi-paragraph docstrings vs +50-70 plan band, within tolerance, mirrors iter-045/047/048 pattern). Plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirms both iter-051 declaration bodies compile end-to-end with kernel-only axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — no new axiom introduced. Iter-051 sorry trajectory `9 → 9` (no transient — bodies probe-confirmed). Phase A iterations-remaining revised UP from ~4 to ~5 (substantive iter-052+ comparison theorem + basic-open acyclicity forms a 4-6-iteration cohort; previous estimate underweighted).
5	
6	## Stages
7	- [x] init
8	- [x] autoformalize
9	- [ ] prover
10	- [ ] polish
11	
12	## Big-picture context
13	
14	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
15	
16	`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (Serre finiteness on a proper curve via the Mayer-Vietoris LES + affine Čech vanishing) has been the iter-009 → iter-050 push, building the `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology infrastructure end-to-end through (a) the iter-009/010/014/015 H⁰ algebraic bridges, (b) the iter-016 → iter-026 verbatim Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` mirror (split iter-027 into a dedicated file), (c) the iter-028 → iter-037 cover-totality bridge, (d) the iter-038/039 H⁰ Module.Finite transports, (e) the iter-040/041 (mis-designed)/042/043 affine + wholespace H⁰/H>0 carrier predicates, (f) the iter-044 geometric Stein input, (g) the iter-045 global-sections evaluation iso, (h) the iter-046 producer chain end-to-end for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` (8 declarations), (i) the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure (4 declarations), (j) the iter-048 Čech-side acyclicity carrier predicate (3 declarations), (k) the iter-049 top-supremum HModule transport (2 declarations; chains iter-048 with iter-034's universe bridge to land `Subsingleton (HModule k F n)` at universe `u+1` whenever `⨆𝒰 = ⊤`), and (l) the iter-050 comparison-iso typeclass carrier + instance-driven consumers (4 declarations; packages the iter-052+ comparison hypothesis as a typeclass for instance synthesis). **Iter-051 (this iteration)** appends the symmetric instance-driven supremum HModule' consumer + curve specialisation (2 declarations, ~30-50 LOC). This completes the typeclass-driven design symmetry: iter-049/050 covers the top-supremum case at universe `u+1` (genus carrier path); iter-051 covers the intermediate-supremum case at universe `u` (the exact form iter-053+ needs to instantiate `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` for an affine open via a basic-open cover with `⨆𝒰 = U ≠ ⊤`).
17	
18	## Iteration 051 plan-agent summary (this iteration)
19	
20	**Iter-050 verification.** Plan-agent verification (this pass) confirms iter-050 closure landed cleanly:
21	
22	- **Sorry count**: `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary` returns `9 total across 3 file(s)` (5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`). Pre-iter-050 `9`, post-iter-050 `9`, target post-iter-051 prover `9`.
23	- **File diagnostics**: `lean_diagnostic_messages` on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`. Zero warnings, zero errors at the diagnostic level.
24	- **File LOC**: `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` 1165 LOC (iter-050 added 88 LOC = 1077 → 1165; +88 vs +50-70 plan estimate band; slight overage from multi-paragraph docstrings, mirrors iter-045/047/048 pattern; within tolerance).
25	- **Iter-050 declarations placed correctly**: `Scheme.HasCechToHModuleIso` at L1094, `Scheme.cechToHModuleIso` at L1110, `Scheme.subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top` at L1134, `Scheme.subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top_curve` at L1152 — all inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` `section CoverTotality` (matching iter-035-037/049 pattern with `Scheme.` prefix dropped on declared names).
26	- **`Genus.lean` preserved**: still 0 sorries; iter-011 first-protected closure intact.
27	- **`archon-protected.yaml`**: `git diff archon-protected.yaml` returns empty. Unchanged.
28	- **No new axioms**: no new `axiom` declarations in `AlgebraicJacobian/`.
29	- **Iter-050 axiom check**: `lean_verify` on each of the four iter-050 declarations returns `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: [{line: 259, pattern: 'local instance'}]}` — all kernel-only; the `local instance` warning at L259 is pre-existing (predates iter-050's L1094/L1110/L1134/L1152 insertions).
30	- **No prover deviations**: iter-050 prover used the verbatim probe-confirmed bodies from iter-050 PROGRESS.md.
31	
32	**Iter-051 work scope.** With iter-049/050's universe-`u+1` `HModule` instance-driven consumers in scope (top-supremum case), the next strategic step is to **complete the typeclass-driven design symmetry** by adding the analogous instance-driven supremum consumer at universe `u` (intermediate-supremum case, no totality assumption). Currently iter-048's `subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover` requires the comparison iso `compIso : ∀ n, cechCohomology n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' n (⨆ 𝒰)` as an *explicit* argument; this is the only consumer in the typeclass family that still requires explicit `compIso` passing. Iter-051 closes this gap with a thin instance-driven counterpart that takes `[HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰]` (iter-050's typeclass) instead, recovering `compIso` automatically via iter-050's `cechToHModuleIso` extractor.
33	
34	**Why iter-051 is needed.** The iter-053+ producer for `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` needs to conclude `Subsingleton (HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) i U)` for an affine open `U` and `i > 0`. The natural route: pick a basic-open cover `𝒰` of `U` with `⨆𝒰 = U`, instantiate `[IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]` (iter-052+ producer) AND `[HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]` (iter-052+ producer, gated on the substantive comparison theorem), then apply iter-051 (THIS ITERATION) to get `Subsingleton (HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) i (⨆𝒰)) = Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)` (after rewriting via `⨆𝒰 = U`). Subsingleton (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n) :=
  subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top (𝒰 := 𝒰) h n hn

/-- Iter-051: instance-driven supremum HModule' consumer (abstract sheaf form).
Combines iter-048's `IsCechAcyclicCover` carrier with iter-050's `HasCechToHModuleIso`
carrier: under both class hypotheses, conclude `Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))`
for `n > 0`.

Body: applies iter-048's `subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover` directly,
with the comparison iso supplied via the iter-050 extractor `cechToHModuleIso`. The
extractor fires automatically on the `[HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰]` instance argument;
no explicit `compIso` needs to be passed at the call site.

This is the iter-051 instance-driven counterpart to iter-048's explicit-argument
supr form, completing the typeclass-driven design symmetry: iter-049 / iter-050
covers the top-supremum case at universe `u+1` (genus carrier path); iter-051
covers the intermediate-supremum case at universe `u` (used by iter-053+ to
instantiate `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` for an affine open via a basic-open cover
where `⨆𝒰 = U ≠ ⊤`).

In contrast to iter-049 / iter-050's `_top` form, this consumer requires only
single-universe `[HasExt.{u}]` (no `[HasExt.{u+1}]`), since it does not pass through
iter-034's universe bridge. The conclusion stays at universe `u`, on `HModule'` (not
`HModule`). -/
theorem subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso
    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰] [HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰]
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
    Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) :=
  subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover cechToHModuleIso n hn

/-- Iter-051: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.

Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 / iter-048 / iter-049 / iter-050
`_curve` pattern: a thin dot-notation wrapper that saves call sites in the curve
setting (where `F` is the structure sheaf) from re-typing `toModuleKSheaf C`
whenever the iter-051 instance-driven consumer is chained through. Used by the
iter-053+ producer instance for `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. -/
theorem subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_curve
    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    [IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]
    [HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]
    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
    Subsingleton (HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) :=
  subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn

end CoverTotality

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean — iter-051 (prover round)

## Summary

declarations inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` `section CoverTotality`,
after iter-050's `subsingleton_HModule_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_top_curve`,
before `end CoverTotality`.

bodies in PROGRESS.md; file compiles cleanly; both new theorems verify
kernel-only.

## subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Insert the verbatim probe-confirmed body from PROGRESS.md (single Edit, appended just before `end CoverTotality` at L1163). Body delegates to iter-048's `subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover`, with iter-050's `cechToHModuleIso` extractor firing automatically on the `[HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰]` instance argument to recover the explicit `compIso` that iter-048's consumer expects.
- **Result:** RESOLVED.
- **Verification:**
  - `lean_diagnostic_messages` on `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` → `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}` (zero errors, zero warnings at the diagnostic level).
  - `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso` → `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: [{line: 259, pattern: 'local instance'}]}` — kernel-only; the `local instance` warning at L259 is pre-existing.

## subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_curve

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Insert the verbatim probe-confirmed body from PROGRESS.md as the second declaration in the same Edit. The body delegates to the abstract form (declaration 1) with the `(𝒰 := 𝒰)` named-argument syntax, since positional inference is ambiguous via implicit arguments alone.
- **Result:** RESOLVED.
- **Verification:**
  - `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_curve` → `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: [{line: 259, pattern: 'local instance'}]}` — kernel-only.

## Notes / Outcome

- **No new sorry introduced; no sorry removed** (this iteration adds two
  fully-proved consumer theorems, no `sorry` placeholder is touched). Sorry
  count `9 → 9` as predicted in PROGRESS.md.
- **No new axioms introduced.** `Classical.choice` was already in scope since
  iter-048 / iter-050.
- **No new imports added.** All required names
  (`subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover`, `cechToHModuleIso`,
  `IsCechAcyclicCover`, `HasCechToHModuleIso`, `HModule'`, `toModuleKSheaf`)
  were already in scope, as predicted.
- **Single Edit form** used: one append before `end CoverTotality`, exactly as
  specified.
- **`Scheme.` prefix dropped** on declared names per the namespace placement
  rule (mirrors iter-035-037/049/050 pattern).
- **`theorem` not `instance`**: per the rigid declaration shape requirement.
- **Single-universe `[HasExt.{u}]` only**: no `[HasExt.{u+1}]` annotation, in
  contrast to iter-049/050's `_top` form.

## Blueprint markers ready

The two iter-051 declarations are formalized with no `sorry`. The review agent
should mark `\leanok` on both:

- `thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_prime_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso`
- `thm:Scheme_subsingleton_HModule_prime_supr_of_hasCechToHModuleIso_curve`

in `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § *Instance-driven
supremum HModule' consumer (iter-051)*.
